summary: A Docker-based reproduction confirmed that an unauthenticated requester can overwrite a publicly readable editable file through the Saltcorn file editor endpoints.
expected_security_impact: The PoC should demonstrate unauthenticated overwrite of a public editable JavaScript file and emit `EVOHUNT_PUBLIC_FILE_EDITOR_WRITE_CONFIRMED` after the served file body changes to attacker-controlled content.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned Saltcorn tag `v1.6.0-beta.1`, built the reproduction image, installed the target dependencies, and printed `EVOHUNT_PUBLIC_FILE_EDITOR_WRITE_CONFIRMED`. The exploit observed `editPageStatus=200`, `saveStatus=302`, `saveRedirect=/files?dir=.`, `targetFile=public-editable-735-1780307804888.js`, and `servedBodyAfterOverwrite="console.log('pwned');\n"`.
verification_method: The automated exploit creates a public JavaScript file, fetches `/files/edit/<file>` without authentication to obtain the CSRF token and session cookie, submits replacement content to `POST /files/edit/<file>`, and then fetches `/files/serve/<file>` to assert the exact overwritten body.
evidence_produced_by_run: Editor save returned HTTP 302 with redirect `/files?dir=.`
Served file body after overwrite was `console.log('pwned');\n`
Target file used during the successful run: `public-editable-735-1780307804888.js`

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
